architectureSummary: Flask follows a modular, layered architecture with clear separation of concerns. At its core, Flask implements the WSGI specification, acting as a bridge between web servers and Python web applications. The framework uses a context-based design with two primary contexts: the application context (containing app-level data like configuration) and the request context (containing request-specific data). These contexts are managed using Python's contextvars module, allowing for thread-safe and async-safe access to global-like objects (current_app, request, session, g) without actual global state. Flask's architecture is split into a sansio layer (pure Python logic, no I/O) and an I/O layer (WSGI functionality and server integration). Both the main Flask app and Blueprints inherit from a common Scaffold base class, providing consistent APIs for routing, error handling, and template registration.

modules.name: Core Application Module
modules.description: Main Flask application class implementing WSGI interface and request handling. Entry point for all Flask apps.

modules.name: Context Management
modules.description: Manages application and request contexts using contextvars for thread-safe global access. Core to Flask's design pattern.

modules.name: Blueprints Module
modules.description: Provides modular application components for organizing routes and resources. Allows splitting a large app into smaller registered components.

modules.name: Configuration Module
modules.description: Handles application configuration from various sources including files, environment variables, and Python objects.

modules.name: Routing & Helpers
modules.description: Utility functions for URL generation, redirects, file sending, and response handling. Used in virtually every Flask view.

modules.name: Request/Response Wrappers
modules.description: Flask-specific request and response objects extending Werkzeug's base classes with additional helpers.

modules.name: Session Management
modules.description: Secure cookie-based session handling with cryptographic signing via ItsDangerous.

modules.name: Templating Module
modules.description: Jinja2 template rendering with Flask-specific context injection and a dispatching loader for blueprints.

modules.name: CLI Module
modules.description: Command-line interface for Flask applications built on Click. Provides `flask run`, `flask shell`, and `flask routes` commands.

modules.name: JSON Module
modules.description: JSON serialization/deserialization with Flask-specific handling including datetime, UUID, and tagged JSON support.

modules.name: Signals Module
modules.description: Event system for decoupled communication between Flask components using Blinker. Fires events on request start/finish, template render, and context push/pop.

modules.name: Testing Module
modules.description: Test client and utilities for testing Flask applications without running a live server.

modules.name: Views Module
modules.description: Class-based views for organizing view logic by HTTP method. Provides View and MethodView base classes.

modules.name: Globals Module
modules.description: Context-local proxy objects for accessing the current app, request, session, and g. These are the famous Flask globals used in every view function.

onboardingGuide:
## Getting Started

1. Begin with README.md — understand Flask's philosophy and minimal example
2. Read src/flask/__init__.py — see the full public API
3. Study src/flask/app.py lines 109–363 — the Flask class initialization
4. Explore examples/tutorial/ — complete working app with best practices

## Key Concepts

1. WSGI Protocol — Flask implements WSGI via wsgi_app() in app.py:1566-1616
2. Context Locals — contextvars powers thread-safe globals; study ctx.py and globals.py
3. Application vs Request Context — two separate lifecycles
4. Sansio Architecture — pure logic in sansio/, I/O wrappers in main modules
5. Scaffold Pattern — common base for Flask and Blueprint in sansio/scaffold.py
6. Signals — event-driven via Blinker in signals.py
7. Routing — Werkzeug routing system wrapped by Flask decorators

## Priority Files

- src/flask/__init__.py (⭐⭐⭐) — public API
- src/flask/app.py (⭐⭐⭐) — Flask class
- src/flask/ctx.py (⭐⭐⭐) — context management
- src/flask/globals.py (⭐⭐⭐) — request/session/g proxies
- src/flask/helpers.py (⭐⭐) — url_for, redirect, abort
- src/flask/wrappers.py (⭐⭐) — Request/Response
- src/flask/blueprints.py (⭐⭐) — modular routes

## Conventions

- Type hints everywhere; from __future__ import annotations at top
- Private functions: _leading_underscore
- Context vars: _cv_ prefix (e.g. _cv_app)
- Tests: pytest in tests/, mirrors source structure
- Async: optional via asgiref; sync-first
- Env vars: FLASK_ prefix (e.g. FLASK_DEBUG)
- Code quality: ruff + mypy + pre-commit hooks
